opp.ais_why:
• Product scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake and AppXtend across S2P + Intake at $500K with extensive S2P modules (eProcurement, iContract, iRisk, iSupplier). The opp name explicitly calls out 'Intake' which maps to Merlin Intake AI module ‚Äî AI is in the deal name. Three AI-flavoured SKUs (Merlin, Merlin Intake, AppXtend) is a strong product-level AI signal.
• Engaged BD, Shortlisted stage. Prior run captured 12 Avoma meetings including multi-hour workshops with Karin Eppich, Engelbert Poelki, and EY advisor Christian Buschhueter on the Austrian Post side. LastActivityDate 13 May 2026 confirms active engagement. EY-advised RFP process plus the Engaged BD plus the dual AI products plus opp-name-embedded Intake is a multi-signal AI Hungry profile.
• SF record was too large to fully fetch this run; the Avoma query (date-narrowed) returned an Etex Group meeting under a different oppid so couldn't re-verify the meeting transcripts. Classification leans on Engaged + Shortlisted + dual AI products (Merlin + Merlin Intake) + EY partner advisory + opp-name 'Intake'. Medium confidence pending direct AI-keyword evidence from a verified meeting transcript.

items.area: Exec access to the economic buyer (CPO / board)
items.quote: buyer requested a management meeting with the CPO to lock terms; Zycus has no direct exec-to-exec session yet
feasibility unverified pending WPS version confirmation and vendor API details
no savings or ROI numbers to win the internal budget ranking
CIO has not committed SAP resources or a start date; blocked by HANA and Turkey

metrics.narrative: The value story is qualitative, not quantified. The group procurement lead was explicit on 1 Jul that Austrian Post is already highly digitalized and therefore 'there is no real sense of urgency to change everything', and that the business cases 'are fighting against each other' for internal budget, so procurement must prove value to rank the investment. Group spend is about 1 billion EUR across roughly 35 procurement staff, and the upside is AI/agentic automation plus a unified S2P suite replacing the aging WPS landscape, but no ROI, savings, or cycle-time numbers have been put on the table. Quantifying the business case is the highest-value MEDDPICC action.

champion.narrative: There is a strong internal champion network. Pölki, the strategic buyer and project co-lead, described the current move as 'unser Strohhalm von der Melissa, von der Karin und von mir, dass wir das Ding hier einfangen können' (25 Jun), i.e. he, Karin Eppich and Melissa Koller are actively trying to lock Zycus in. Karin Eppich (system-side project lead) expressed a preference for Zycus on 16 Mar and drives the technical workstream. What is still missing is not advocacy but air cover: the champions cannot commit budget or IT resources, so they need Zycus to arm them with an ROI case and an IT-resource story to carry upward.

competition.narrative: This is a live head-to-head with Coupa: on 1 Jul the Zycus rep noted the buyer had run similar API/integration conversations with Coupa, and on 25 Jun the rep characterized it as a 'Kopf-an-Kopf-Rennen' where Coupa also turns flexible on commercials. Zycus leads on function and AI per the 11 Jun POC feedback, and is inside the stated budget, so the wedge is commercial flexibility plus the agentic-AI edge, not features. GEP is on the shortlist but invisible on calls, and an orchestration-overlay path (Tronkeon-style bolt-on to avoid SAP effort) tempts IT. The single strongest current threat is not a rival vendor but do-nothing/deferral driven by the IT resource gate and an internal budget fight, made sharper by the board putting its biggest logistics project on hold.

sources: Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1 and Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026
identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is an aging, fragmented procurement landscape: the incumbent WPS / Allocation Network P2P (acquired by QAT) has poor post-acquisition support and the buyer is on an older WPS4 build, with the buying team surprised by how far behind it sits. The buyer wants one interconnected S2P suite with AI and a partner that brings use-case ideas, and it wants to eliminate its separate procurement information system, which Zycus S2C can replace. The countervailing reality, stated by the group procurement lead, is that high existing digitalization means urgency is low, so the pain owner (procurement, Pölki and Koller) must win an internal budget fight to act. The SAP resource constraint is the operational blocker to realizing the change.

sources: Task: Zycus MSA und SLAs, 18 May 2026
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are underway but not closed: Zycus shared the MSA (Rahmenvertrag), a standard Order Form and SLAs on 18 May, and the parties are shaping a 7-year S2C contract with the price guaranteed for two years after signature and procurement carved out as an optional later phase. Implementation cost and SOW still depend on IT scoping and EY, which have not happened. No legal redlines have started, and it is a license-only commercial (implementation quoted separately once scope is agreed). The compelling event is the buyer's own stated urgency to sign S2C this year to hit its target, not a contract-expiration date.

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority sits above the project team: the board (Vorstand) ranks investments on the digital roadmap, and the buyer has asked for a management meeting with the CPO (referenced as Herr Flandorfer in the deal next steps) to lock commercial flexibility. On the Zycus side the commercial conversation is carried by Amit (who ran the 1 Jul pricing session) and by the owner's manager, and pricing was escalated to Zycus's founder. The group procurement lead personally joined the 1 Jul commercial call and now owns the flexibility ask, which raises seniority, but Zycus has not yet secured a direct exec-to-exec meeting with the CPO or board. Securing that CPO session is the path to written sign-off.

sources: Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1 and Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026
decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence: EY-advised RFP with a commercial round (top 4, then shortlist), POC completed 6-13 May, and now commercial negotiation moving toward management sign-off. Approvals run through the board's digital-roadmap ranking and a management/CPO meeting the buyer requested for the next week. Implementation is gated by the CIO committing SAP resources (blocked by the HANA migration and the Turkey rollout), and EY leads the implementation, so the paper and scoping path involves EY plus IT. A recurring 'Blocker' meeting on Wednesdays is the buyer's internal working cadence to converge.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented from the EY-advised RFP, the POC, and the commercial rounds: functional S2C and P2P fit, agentic-AI capability (Merlin), price transparency and stability over time, commercial flexibility for a phased rollout, SAP/WPS integration effort, apples-to-apples comparability versus other vendors, implementation cost, and training coverage. Pölki stated price transparency and security over time matter most and that he needs a side-by-side comparison of the Coupa and Zycus offers (Next_Step, 21 May 2026). On 1 Jul the buyer stressed comparing 'prices for functionality with prices for functionality' rather than agent-by-agent, and valued that Zycus trains 30 users versus fewer seats elsewhere.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 68.
blocks.read: We're in it, with a slight edge.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Pölki (project co-lead and strategic buyer) and Karin Eppich (system-side lead), with Melissa Koller, are actively pushing internally to lock Zycus in; on 25 Jun Pölki called the current move their lifeline to secure the deal. They advocate strongly but cannot commit budget or IT resources, so the missing piece is an ROI case and an IT-resource story to carry upward.
bullets.text: Champion is driving it — Pölki (project co-lead and strategic buyer) and Karin Eppich (system-side lead), with Melissa Koller, are actively pushing internally to lock Zycus…

blocks.read: There's a live competitive threat plus execution risk — both below.
blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Ranked by current threat: do-nothing / deferral is the biggest threat (IT SAP resources blocked by HANA and Turkey, plus an internal budget fight after the board put its largest logistics project on hold, 25 Jun-1 Jul).

bullets.text: Close date 23 Jul not credible; buyer named two start scenarios (Q4 2026 or 2027) and deferred P2P to 2028
SAP resources blocked by the HANA migration and Turkey rollout; CIO has not committed and WPS4 API feasibility is unverified
Weak urgency due to high existing digitalization; internal budget fight worsened by the board putting the biggest logistics project on hold
Agentic-AI event/user volumes drive Zycus cost; buyer pushed volumes up ~5x while wanting the price held

items: Convene the CPO commercial-alignment meeting to lock the 7-year S2C terms and phased flexibility. (by 10 Jul)
Give the CIO the AI iSaaS SAP-integration proof to free IT resources for a 2026 start. (by 11 Jul)
Build a quantified ROI case versus the WPS and eProc landscape for Pölki to win the budget ranking. (by 16 Jul)
blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 68 is carried by momentum 70 vs stage-expected 56; preference strength +1.00 (weight 20). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 70 (moving forward): seniority rising (from call evidence). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 52 is anchored on the stage win (68), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 70, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative only: replace aging WPS, unify S2P, add agentic AI; buyer admits weak urgency and no ROI numbers, business cases fighting for budget.

items.detail: Close date 23 Jul not credible; buyer named two start scenarios (Q4 2026 or 2027) and deferred P2P to 2028
SAP resources blocked by the HANA migration and Turkey rollout; CIO has not committed and WPS4 API feasibility is unverified
Weak urgency due to high existing digitalization; internal budget fight worsened by the board putting the biggest logistics project on hold
Agentic-AI event/user volumes drive Zycus cost; buyer pushed volumes up ~5x while wanting the price held
Coupa is a live head-to-head running parallel integration talks and matching commercial flexibility

reasons.detail: On the 25 Jun pricing call the buyer explicitly asked to move commercial talks 'auf Management Ebene' to secure flexibility, and pricing has already been escalated to Zycus's founder. Champion Poelki and Eppich want a CEO-to-CPO (Flandorfer) session to lock the proposed 7-year S2C + Procurement price-lock and phased terms; Zycus has not yet secured that exec-to-exec meeting. Without founder sign-off on the flexibility and a peer CPO conversation, this $448K forecasted Upside Key Deal (close Jul 23) stalls on commercials while Coupa runs parallel commercial and integration talks.
reasons.ceo_ask: Approve the escalated 7-year price-lock/flexibility and open a peer line to CPO Flandorfer to close the commercial gap above the project team.
reasons.summary: Founder must lock the 7-year price flexibility the buyer escalated to management
reasons.ceo_action: Personally take the CPO (Flandorfer) commercial-alignment meeting and approve the escalated 7-year S2C price-lock and phased flexibility.
reasons.why_not_vp: The commercial flexibility exceeds normal discount authority and has already been escalated to Zycus's founder, and the buyer is asking for a board/CPO-level counterpart to lock a strategic 7-year term - a peer relationship a VP/CRO cannot substitute for.

reasons.detail: The close date was pushed from Jun 30 to Jul 23 (22 Jun) and the amount cut from $650K to $448,680 (25 Jun). On the 1 Jul onsite the buyer floated two timelines - start S2C this year or defer to mid-2027 - after the board put its largest logistics project on hold and an internal budget fight opened, with IT SAP resources blocked by HANA/Turkey. If IT stays gated and procurement loses the budget ranking, this Upside Key Deal defers a full year despite Zycus's strong function/AI lead.
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Dirk whether Austrian Post's board budget freeze and IT resource gate will slip S2C to 2027, and whether the AI iSaaS integration proof is on track to unblock a 2026 start.
reasons.summary: Key $448K deal slipping: close pushed 23 days, board floats 2027 deferral
reasons.evidence: zwei moegliche Timelines: start S2C this year or mid-2027 ... jede Woche zaehlt; and the biggest logistics project was just put on hold by the board (Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1/2, 1 Jul 2026)

critical_signals.text: The 1 July onsite with the group procurement lead went well and converged on a 7-year S2C contract with procurement carved out as optional.
Coupa is in a neck-and-neck race and running parallel integration talks, though Austrian Post rates Zycus stronger on function and AI after the May POC.
The head of group procurement joined the commercial call for the first time and now personally owns the flexibility ask.
The buyer now wants a full scenario cost comparison spanning current systems, IT effort and timelines before it will commit.
Price sits inside the stated 400-500K budget, but IT resources blocked by the SAP HANA and Turkey rollout are the real barrier to signing this year.

items: In-person onsite held 1 Jul with the group procurement lead and the commercial owner (Amit)
Buyer stated intent to sign S2C this year and that every week counts
7-year contract structure and phased rollout being actively shaped
confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and warming: in-person onsite 1 Jul, senior procurement lead engaged, commercials converging, buyer pushing to sign this year. Not cooling.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close ~20 (close 2026-07-23, today 2026-07-03). Stage Shortlisted since 2026-04-30, ~64 days in stage. Onsite commercial alignment held 1 Jul with the group procurement lead; pricing converging (7-year S2C, price guaranteed 2 years, procurement optional from 2028/2029). Remaining before signature: IT/CIO resource commitment, implementation scoping with EY, management/CPO sign-off, legal on the MSA/Orderform already shared 18 May. On 1 Jul the buyer said it could not give an exact timeline and that every week counts to sign S2C this year; realistic signature is Q4 2026, not 23 Jul. Deal is healthy and engaged, the sole issue is the date.
evidence: 1 Jul onsite (Teil 1 and Teil 2): group procurement lead joined the commercial call; both sides agreed to keep S2C core as-is, carve out the procurement/P2P module as optional (start 2028/2029), and hold the price with reworked agentic-AI volumes (source: Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1 and Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026)
Buyer: 'eigentlich hätten wir jetzt schon zu schreiben wollen ... jede Woche zählt mittlerweile' (Pölki, Zycus Onsite Teil 1, 1 Jul 2026) shows intent to sign S2C this year

Buyer could not commit a firm timeline: 'zwei mögliche Timelines: start S2C this year or mid-2027', procurement/P2P deferred to 2028 (Matthias, group procurement lead, Zycus Onsite Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026)

IT resource gate persists: signing S2C this year still requires the CIO to free SAP resources that are blocked by the HANA migration and the Turkey SAP rollout (Zycus Onsite Teil 1 and Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026)

Buyer requested a management meeting with the CPO (referenced as Herr Flandorfer in the deal next steps) to lock commercial flexibility and terms (Pricing Structure call, 25 Jun 2026; reconfirmed 1 Jul)
POC completed 6-13 May with positive functional and AI feedback (POC feedback call, 11 Jun 2026)
Amount reduced 650,000 to 448,680 on 25 Jun reflecting the procurement carve-out; within the buyer's stated 400-500K SaaS budget (SF field history 25 Jun; budget stated 24 Mar per Next_Step)

Forecast Upside Key Deal on a 23 Jul close is not defensible: no management/CPO sign-off, no IT resource commitment, no contract redlines started. Recommended: Pipeline until IT commits and the CPO approves.
north_star_verdict.headline: Functionally preferred after a strong POC and a good 1 Jul onsite where commercials converged on a 7-year S2C core with procurement carved out as optional, but the 23 Jul close is not credible: the buyer named two possible start scenarios (Q4 2026 or 2027) and IT resources stay blocked by the SAP HANA and Turkey rollout.

flags: Treat this as a commercial-flexibility race with Coupa, not a feature race: lead with the 7-year S2C price-lock and phased rollout, backed by the confirmed POC functional and AI edge.
The deal is gated by IT resources, not procurement's preference: get the CIO the AI iSaaS SAP-integration proof so the ~170-to-30-day effort stops blocking a 2026 start.
Procurement must win an internal budget fight against an on-hold logistics mega-project: arm Pölki, Eppich and Koller with a quantified ROI versus the aging WPS landscape.
best_practice_check.summary: Win levers grounded in a live Coupa head-to-head, an IT resource gate, and an internal budget fight.

customer_preference.evidence: Pölki (25 Jun): the current move is 'unser Strohhalm ... das Ding hier einfangen' - he, Eppich and Koller are actively trying to lock Zycus in; POC feedback favourable vs competitors.

competitive_position.summary: Ranked by current threat: do-nothing / deferral is the biggest threat (IT SAP resources blocked by HANA and Turkey, plus an internal budget fight after the board put its largest logistics project on hold, 25 Jun-1 Jul). Coupa is the live external head-to-head, running parallel integration talks and matching on commercial flexibility, though Zycus leads on function and AI after the 11 Jun POC. An orchestration-overlay (Tronkeon-style) bolt-on tempts IT as the low-effort path. GEP remains a shortlisted but invisible name. Incumbent WPS / Allocation Network is being displaced and is dormant as a threat.

competitors.name: Do-nothing / deferral (IT resource gate + internal budget fight)
competitors.quote: zwei mögliche Timelines: start S2C this year or mid-2027 ... jede Woche zählt; and the biggest logistics project was just put on hold by the board
competitors.source: Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1 and Teil 2, 1 Jul 2026; Pricing Structure call 25 Jun 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Remove IT's rationale with the AI iSaaS SAP-integration proof (cut ~170 to ~30 days) and arm procurement with a quantified ROI to win the internal budget ranking.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: ich hör da raus, dass Sie da ähnliches Gespräch auch schon mit den Kollegen von Coupa hatten (buyer confirms parallel API talks with Coupa)
competitors.source: Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1, 1 Jul 2026; Pricing Structure call 25 Jun 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Functionally preferred and within budget (448,680 vs Coupa's 475K); counter Coupa's commercial flexibility with the 7-year price-lock and the integrated agentic-AI edge, backed by the POC result.

competitors.name: Orchestration overlay (Tronkeon-style bolt-on)
competitors.quote: other players offer orchestration tools as an overlay on the existing P2P to avoid SAP integration; attractive to IT but weak on reporting, platform benefits and transformation
competitors.how_we_win: Prove Zycus SAP integration is low-effort via AI iSaaS and show the overlay's reporting/AI gaps; offer Zycus Intake bridged to the current P2P as an interim.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: GEP is in the RFP shortlist (top 3-4) but has not surfaced on any buyer call or POC discussion
competitors.how_we_win: Maintain the functional and AI differentiation and lock IT commitment before GEP can re-engage.

competitors.name: WPS / Allocation Network (incumbent P2P)
competitors.quote: aging WPS build (WPS4), poor support after the QAT acquisition; buyer wants to replace it
competitors.source: Avoma Zycus Onsite Teil 1, 1 Jul 2026; system demo 19 May 2025
competitors.how_we_win: Baseline to beat, not a competitor; demonstrate full-suite benefits and Intake replacing the WPS front door.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The agentic-AI narrative is landing: Zycus is positioned as the AI leader (outcome-based agents vs bolted-on point agents), the buyer increased agentic event/user volumes 50-70%, and Merlin/Merlin Intake sit at the centre of the S2P scope and the opp name.

items.quote: S2C functionality judged stronger than competitors after the POC
significant interest in the Merlin agentic-AI front; buyer increased sourcing-event volumes
important is price transparency and security over time; needs a side-by-side vs Coupa
Zycus carved out procurement as optional and offered a 7-year S2C with a 2-year price guarantee
WPS4 API feasibility unverified; SAP resources blocked by HANA and Turkey
implementation cost needs IT scoping and EY; only license price agreed so far

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong fit on function and AI, converging on commercials, but exposed on integration effort and implementation-cost clarity that depend on IT.

hard.ais_why:
• Product scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake and AppXtend across S2P + Intake at $500K with extensive S2P modules (eProcurement, iContract, iRisk, iSupplier). The opp name explicitly calls out 'Intake' which maps to Merlin Intake AI module ‚Äî AI is in the deal name. Three AI-flavoured SKUs (Merlin, Merlin Intake, AppXtend) is a strong product-level AI signal.
• Engaged BD, Shortlisted stage. Prior run captured 12 Avoma meetings including multi-hour workshops with Karin Eppich, Engelbert Poelki, and EY advisor Christian Buschhueter on the Austrian Post side. LastActivityDate 13 May 2026 confirms active engagement. EY-advised RFP process plus the Engaged BD plus the dual AI products plus opp-name-embedded Intake is a multi-signal AI Hungry profile.
• SF record was too large to fully fetch this run; the Avoma query (date-narrowed) returned an Etex Group meeting under a different oppid so couldn't re-verify the meeting transcripts. Classification leans on Engaged + Shortlisted + dual AI products (Merlin + Merlin Intake) + EY partner advisory + opp-name 'Intake'. Medium confidence pending direct AI-keyword evidence from a verified meeting transcript.

Engagement timeline:

﻿- Apr 16, 2026 - Call with Post IT scheduled for 22.04. - first discussion for IT to understand the efforts and timelines, no technical discussion

﻿- Apr 13, 2026 - Shared information about Certinal pricing with E.Pölki and requested list of countries for QES

﻿- Apr 13, 2026 - Introduced Dirk per E-Mail

- Mar 31, 2026 - Karin Eppich scheduled onsite sessions for POC

- Mar 30, 2026 - P2P POC Use Case review with ÖPAG completed

- Mar 27, 2026 - S2C POC Use Case review with ÖPAG completed

- Mar 25, 2026 - Submitted commercial offer via ÖPAG supplier portal

- Mar 24, 2026 - Talked on the phone with E. Pölki to validate the budget, they expect SaaS in range of 400-500K.

- Mar 19, 2026 - Talked on the phone with E. Pölki - he mentioned that there will be further pricing discussions and this round is about first price level comparison between the top 4 suppliers.

- Mar 18, 2026 - Post AG published the commercial round of RFP

- Mar 16, 2026 - Call with Karin Eppich - she expressed her preference for Zycus

- Mar 16, 2026 - Post AG requested a call to discuss testing cases

- Mar 11, 2026 - Submitted ISO27001 on request of Post AG

- Mar 09, 2026 - RFP response submitted within deadline

- Feb 27, 2026 - Sorted access to the portal and confirmed participation in RFP.

- Feb 24, 2026 - Received invitation to RFP. Struggling with access to the portal.

- Feb 22, 2026 - Post reached out asking for confirmation if we can implement P2P for their Turkish legal unit in 2026 (confirmed, provided decision until June 2026). Decision making is from Austria and it will be single contract for Austria and Turkey.

- 16.12.2025 - Met the post team and scheduled onsite workshop

- 11.12.2025 - Scheduled personal meeting with E. Pölki and Team on 16.12. in Vienna

- 10.12.2025 - Proposed dates for onsite workshop : 20-21.01.

- 09.12.2025 - Shared updated price indication. Reduced deal size (the budget does not allow big bang and they will start with less users)

- 05.12.2025 - Reviewed pricing with E. Pölki and received new inputs

- 26.11.2025 - Post requested pricing for company-wide implementation (to be shared by 4.12. the latest)40
